narratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
narratives.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western (specifically American) foreign policy and perception, portraying it as hypocritical and alarmist by claiming it unjustly frames Russia as a constant threat when it is 'SELDOM a threat'.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narratives.evidence_quote: thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narratives.reasoning: This statement highlights a negative outcome (being 'the worst of the worst of humanity') attributed directly to the actions of Western governments and their unelected officials.

narratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narratives.reasoning: This quote explicitly questions the reliability and motives of Western media, accusing it of harmful misrepresentation ('framing the war') that prolongs conflict.

narratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narratives.reasoning: This statement evokes fear and anxiety about the potential for nuclear war, presenting it as an almost desirable outcome due to despair, which amplifies fears of catastrophic escalation.

narratives.evidence_quote: Russia is Winning, Nuclear Threat is a Sign of Desperation & Very Serious
narratives.reasoning: The title of the post and the core premise of the interview speculates on the current outcome of the war ('Russia is Winning') and the potential consequences of nuclear threats.

In the context of the interview description and the commenter's agreement with Ritter's analysis that 'Russia is Winning,' praising Ritter for 'talking the truth' implicitly endorses and praises the pro-Russian perspective he is presenting.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is overreacting
subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly accuses the US (and by extension the West) of consistently and disproportionately exaggerating threats from Russia and China, which aligns with the definition of portraying the West's reaction as disproportionate.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote suggests the Western media's framing is deliberately prolonging the war, implying the West's primary interest is not Ukraine's well-being but rather continuing the conflict for its own purposes, causing more Ukrainian death.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Diplomacy does/will not work
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: The statement implies that the current diplomatic avenue (negotiations) is being undermined and rendered ineffective by Western media's actions, discrediting the potential of ongoing diplomatic efforts.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs... I do not think an amerikan life is worth 1/2 as much as an African (or any other life outside the west) ..they are trash ..not worth the effort
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote contains extreme, dehumanizing rhetoric that discredits the moral character and value of Western (specifically American) citizens and life. subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Sanctions imposed by Western countries will backfire
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote argues that Western (specifically American) demonization and overreaction to Russia and China is a persistent, misguided policy. It implies that this aggressive posture, which includes sanctions and confrontation, is based on a false premise ('seldom a threat') and is therefore a self-inflicted negative consequence for the West, aligning with the idea that such actions are counterproductive and backfire.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over …thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote expresses a nihilistic desire for nuclear war as a release from the conflict, explicitly blaming 'un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries”' for driving the situation to this catastrophic point. It supports the parent narrative by portraying Western political structures as leading to an existential negative consequence (global nuclear annihilation) for the West itself.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Distrust towards Media: Western media is an instrument of propaganda
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Another must listen to interview with information that you are missing from the corporation propaganda western media
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly labels Western media as 'corporation propaganda,' framing it as a tool that withholds or distorts information, which aligns with the definition of discrediting Western media institutions as instruments of propaganda.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement supports the parent narrative of distrust towards media by accusing Western media of a specific, harmful misrepresentation ('framing the war as if Russia is losing') that has dire consequences (prolonging war, causing death). It discredits Western media but does not fit the more specific definitions of being an 'instrument of propaganda' or targeting Ukrainian media, thus it is a remainder supporting the general narrative.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly evokes fear and anxiety about the use of nuclear weapons, framing a full nuclear war as a plausible and imminent outcome, which aligns with the subnarrative definition.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
subnarratives.reasoning: This question from the text's description amplifies war-related fear by speculating about the use of nuclear weapons as a desperate measure, supporting the parent narrative. However, it is presented as a speculative question rather than a definitive statement of possibility, making it distinct from the more direct evidence used for the specific 'nuclear weapons will be employed' subnarrative. This constitutes a remainder that supports the parent narrative but is not captured by the specific definition.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Speculating war outcomes: Ukrainian army is collapsing
subnarratives.reasoning: The title directly states that Russia is winning, which implies the opposing force, the Ukrainian army, is losing or collapsing in its ability to resist effectively.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going? Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote speculates on the motivations and potential desperate actions (like using nuclear weapons) of one side to alter the war's outcome, which fits the parent narrative of speculating on war outcomes but does not align with the specific definitions about army collapse or loss of territory.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Praise of Russia: Russia is a guarantor of peace and prosperity
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Former Intelligence Officer and Expert on Weapons of Mass Destruction, Scott Ritter, joins the program to give us a better understanding of what is really going on in Ukraine. Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote frames the Western media's portrayal of Russian losses as a harmful fiction that prolongs conflict, implying that accepting Russia's position (presumably winning) would lead to quicker peace, thus portraying Russia as a potential source of stability and an end to death.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion..
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement directly praises Russia by countering the Western 'boogie man' narrative, arguing that Russia has been historically and unjustly maligned as a threat, which is a positive portrayal that supports the parent narrative but does not fit a more specific subnarrative definition.
